info.title: Project Beta API
info.description:
AI-powered content generation platform API.

## Authentication

The API supports three authentication methods:

### 1. httpOnly Cookie (Recommended)

The preferred method for browser-based clients. Call `POST /api/v2/sessions` with credentials to receive httpOnly cookies containing JWT access and refresh tokens.

```
POST /api/v2/sessions
Content-Type: application/json

{"email": "user@example.com", "password": "..."}

→ Set-Cookie: access_token=<jwt>; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Lax
→ Set-Cookie: refresh_token=<jwt>; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Lax
→ Set-Cookie: csrf_token=<token>; Secure; SameSite=Lax
```

### 2. Bearer Token

Pass a JWT token in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer <jwt_token>
```

### 3. API Key

For server-to-server integrations, pass an API key in the `X-API-Key` header:

```
X-API-Key: <your_api_key>
```

Manage API keys at `POST /api/v2/api-keys`.

## CSRF Protection

When using cookie-based authentication, state-changing requests (POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) require a CSRF token:

1. On login, the server sets a `csrf_token` cookie (readable by JavaScript)
2. Include the cookie value in the `X-CSRF-Token` header on every state-changing request
3. The server validates the header matches the cookie (double-submit pattern)

**Exempt endpoints**: Registration, login, password reset, health checks, and Pub/Sub push endpoints.

## API Keys

For programmatic access, use an API key passed via the `X-API-Key` header.
Manage keys at `GET /api/v2/api_keys`.

## API Versioning

All endpoints are prefixed with `/api/v2`. The current API version is also
returned in the `X-API-Version` response header on every request.

## API Conventions

- **Resource-oriented URLs** following Richardson Maturity Model Level 2
- **Standard HTTP methods**: GET (read), POST (create), PUT (replace), PATCH (update), DELETE (remove)
- **Consistent status codes**: 200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 403, 404, 409, 500
- **camelCase** response fields (snake_case converted automatically)

## Rate Limits

| Category | Limit | Scope |
|----------|-------|-------|
| General | 100 requests/minute | Per user (JWT uid) or IP |
| Content generation | 10 requests/hour | Per user or IP |
| Registration | 5 requests/minute | Per IP |
| Login | 10 requests/minute | Per IP |
| Forgot password | 3 requests/minute | Per IP |

Rate limit headers are included in **all** responses:

| Header | Description | Example |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `X-RateLimit-Limit` | Max requests allowed in the current window | `100` |
| `X-RateLimit-Remaining` | Requests remaining in the current window | `87` |
| `X-RateLimit-Reset` | UTC epoch seconds when the window resets | `1710600000` |
| `X-RateLimit-Policy` | Which rate limit policy was applied | `general` |

When the limit is exceeded, a `429 Too Many Requests` response is returned with a `Retry-After` header indicating seconds until the next request is allowed.

## Pagination

List endpoints support pagination via query parameters:
- `page` — Page number (default: 1)
- `page_size` — Items per page (default: 20, max: 100)

## Error Responses

All errors follow a consistent format:

```json
{
  "detail": "Human-readable error message"
}
```

| Status | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| 400 | Validation error or bad input |
| 401 | Missing or invalid authentication |
| 403 | Insufficient permissions |
| 404 | Resource not found |
| 409 | Conflict (e.g. duplicate resource) |
| 429 | Rate limit exceeded |
| 500 | Internal server error |

## SDK Generation

The OpenAPI spec at `/openapi.json` is formatted for automatic SDK generation.
Use [openapi-generator](https://openapi-generator.tech/) or similar tools
to generate typed client libraries.

## Environments

| Environment | Frontend | API |
|-------------|----------|-----|
| Development | `https://dev.matchpointai.com` | Cloud Run (dev) |
| Staging | `https://staging.matchpointai.com` | Cloud Run (staging) |
| Production | `https://app.matchpointai.com` | Cloud Run (prod) |

get.description:
Get brand information.

Returns the brand details and status.
Returns 404 if brand doesn't exist.

RMM-2 Compliant Pattern:
- Use GET /brands/{brand_id} for basic brand data
- Use GET /brands/{brand_id}?include=files to explicitly request file URLs
- Replaces non-compliant pattern: GET /brands/{brand_id}/with-files (hyphenated segment)

Query Parameters:
- include: Optional comma-separated list of additional data to include
          Supported values: 'files' (includes brand_guidelines_url, logo_url)
          Example: ?include=files

Note: File URLs (brand_guidelines_url, logo_url) are always included in the
response for backward compatibility. The include parameter is provided to
demonstrate RMM-2 best practices using query parameters instead of hyphenated
path segments.

Issue #1662: Added authorization check to prevent IDOR vulnerability.
Issue #3083: Added data access audit logging for brand data.

post.description:
Start knowledge discovery workflow for a brand.

Publishes a crawl request to the brand-crawler Pub/Sub topic.
The crawler orchestrates website crawling, content extraction, and
knowledge storage. This is an explicit trigger following RMM Level 2 principles.

The crawl runs asynchronously. Poll the status endpoint to monitor progress.

Design Pattern (RMM L2):
- POST creates a workflow resource (not an RPC "scrape" action)
- Returns 202 Accepted (async processing)
- Provides workflow_id for status polling
- Idempotent: multiple POSTs return existing workflow if already running

**RMM L2 Compliance**: POST /api/v2/brands/{brand_id}/summaries
- Summaries modeled as a resource collection
- POST creates a new summary generation request
- Returns 202 Accepted with correlation info

Summary is generated asynchronously by agentic brand-crawler:
- The agentic service extracts brand assets and generates summaries
- Includes: mission, vision, values, tone/voice, persona, industry, vertical
- Excludes: brand colors, service lists, location
- Brand record is updated with summary when complete

get.description:
Get the status of brand knowledge extraction (Phase 1: Progressive Brand Knowledge).

Returns the current status and progress of the brand's knowledge extraction workflow,
including whether quick extraction and deep crawl phases are complete.

This endpoint enables the frontend to:
- Show instant brand creation feedback
- Display real-time progress of knowledge extraction
- Determine when to enable features that require knowledge data
- Poll for updates during background processing

Phase Architecture:
- Phase 1 (Instant): Brand created in < 1 second with status "not_started"
- Phase 2 (Quick): Quick extraction adds colors/industry in < 3 seconds
- Phase 3 (Deep): Deep crawl runs in background (minutes to hours)

get.description:
Liveness probe -- lightweight database write verification.

Performs database health check:
1. Writes a test record to verify DB accepts writes
2. Reads it back to verify data persistence
3. Deletes it to clean up

Returns:
    200 OK with {"status": "healthy", ...} if all checks pass
    503 Service Unavailable with error details if any check fails

Lightweight liveness probe -- always returns 200 OK.

No dependency checks. Confirms the API process is running and
can handle HTTP requests. Suitable for container orchestration
liveness probes where you only need to know the process is alive.

Returns:
    200 OK with {"status": "alive"} -- always succeeds

Readiness probe -- checks DB + Redis + Pub/Sub.

Each dependency check is independent and wrapped in try/except.
One failing dependency does not crash the entire check.

Returns:
    200 OK with {"status": "ready"} if all core deps are healthy
    503 Service Unavailable with {"status": "not_ready"} if any is down

Full dependency health check -- DB, Pub/Sub, Redis, and external APIs.

Checks ALL downstream service dependencies with individual timeouts.
Returns structured JSON with per-dependency status, latency, and errors.

Dependencies checked: database, redis, pubsub, openrouter, fal, gcs, firecrawl.

Returns:
    200 OK when all dependencies are healthy
    503 Service Unavailable when any dependency is unhealthy

Dependency health endpoint -- checks ALL external dependencies.

For debugging and observability only. NOT for probes.
Includes slow external API checks (OpenRouter, FAL, Firecrawl, GCS).
Each check has an individual timeout.

Returns:
    200 OK with all dependency statuses (always 200 for debugging)

Deep health check for deployment validation.

Checks ALL dependencies plus migration status, SendGrid API key
validity, and OAuth credential configuration. Categorizes each
dependency as critical or non-critical.

Status logic:
- "healthy": all dependencies pass
- "degraded": only non-critical dependencies fail
- "unhealthy": any critical dependency fails

Returns:
    200 OK when all dependencies are healthy
    200 OK with "degraded" status when only non-critical deps fail
    503 Service Unavailable when any critical dependency is unhealthy

Comprehensive diagnostics endpoint for monitoring tools.

Returns structured JSON with per-component status (healthy/degraded/down),
response times, queue depth, processing latency, and cache health
including hit rate and memory usage.

Returns:
    200 OK when all components are healthy
    200 OK with "degraded" when only non-critical deps fail
    503 Service Unavailable when critical components are unhealthy

Update a post's experience prompt and trigger full content regeneration
        through Content Designer interpretation.

        The experience prompt is a user-facing description of the entire post.
        When edited, the backend sends it to Content Designer for interpretation
        into text_prompt and media_prompt, then triggers regeneration of both
        text and media.

        Returns 202 Accepted immediately. Use the status_url to poll for completion.

        **Flow:**
        1. Updates experience_prompt in database
        2. Publishes to Content Designer for interpretation
        3. Content Designer generates text_prompt + media_prompt
        4. Post Generator creates new text and media
        5. Version request completes when generation finishes

- HTTP verb (POST) indicates create/submit action
- No verbs in URL path

Args:
    request: Email verification request with token

Returns:
    UserResponse with email_verified=True

Raises:
    HTTPException 400: If token is invalid or expired
    HTTPException 500: If verification fails due to server error

Resend email verification email (public endpoint, no auth required).

This endpoint allows unauthenticated users to request a new verification
email. This is necessary because users cannot authenticate until their
email is verified, but they may need to request a new verification email
if the original one expired or was lost.

Security consideration: Always returns success message regardless of
whether the email exists or was sent, to prevent email enumeration attacks.

RMM Level 2 Compliance:
- Resource-based URL: /verifications/resend
- HTTP method (POST) indicates action
- No verbs in URL path (resend is a sub-resource, not a verb)
- Returns generic response for security

Args:
    request: Request containing user email address

Returns:
    ResendVerificationResponse with generic success message

200.description: Request processed (email sent if user exists and is unverified)

post.description:
Create or refresh an authentication session (RMM L2 compliant).

This unified endpoint handles both login and token refresh operations
based on the grant_type parameter in the request body.

Supported grant types:
- "password": Login with email and password
- "refresh_token": Refresh an existing session using refresh token

RMM Level 2 Compliance:
- POST to /sessions creates/refreshes session resource
- HTTP verb (POST) indicates create action
- No verbs in URL path
- Grant type differentiates operation (OAuth2 pattern)

Args:
    request: Session request with grant_type and appropriate fields

Returns:
    SessionResponse with tokens and optionally user profile

Raises:
    HTTPException 400: If required fields are missing or invalid
    HTTPException 401: If authentication/refresh fails
    HTTPException 500: If server error occurs

400.description: Invalid request (missing fields or invalid grant_type)

429.description: Account locked due to too many failed login attempts (Issue #2317)

delete.description:
Delete authentication session (logout).

Invalidates the current user's token by adding it to the blacklist.
After logout, the token will be rejected by the authentication middleware.

Issue #3186: Uses require_auth_for_logout instead of require_auth to
handle cases where the JWT token has expired before the logout request
reaches the API. This ensures server-side session cleanup even with
expired tokens.

Issue #3228: Updated to support cookie-based JWT tokens (Issue #3017).
Previously required an Authorization header; now also accepts tokens
from httpOnly cookies. The token is extracted from cookies first, then
falls back to the Authorization header.

Issue #3235: Logout is now idempotent. If the token is already blacklisted
(e.g., double-logout from two browser tabs or a retry), the endpoint returns
200 immediately rather than 401. The session is already invalidated, which
is exactly the desired post-condition of logout.

RMM Level 2 Compliance:
- DELETE to /sessions deletes the session resource
- HTTP verb (DELETE) indicates delete action
- No verbs in URL path (not /logout, but DELETE /sessions)

Args:
    request: FastAPI request object for cookie access
    firebase_user: Decoded token claims (from auth or expired token)
    credentials: Optional HTTP Bearer token from Authorization header
    token_blacklist_service: Injected token blacklist service
    audit_service: Injected audit service

Returns:
    LogoutResponse with confirmation message

Raises:
    HTTPException 401: If no token provided (cookie or header)
    HTTPException 500: If logout fails due to server error

post.description:
Create a password reset request (RMM Level 2 compliant).

Creates a new password-reset resource and sends an email with reset token

Security consideration: Always returns success message regardless of whether

RMM Level 2 Compliance:
- POST to /passwords creates new password-reset resource
- Resource-based URL (noun, not verb)

Args:

put.description:

Updates the password-reset resource identified by token, setting the

RMM Level 2 Compliance:

- New password in request body
- Resource-based URL structure

Args:

    request: Password reset update request with new password

Returns:
    PasswordResetUpdateResponse with success message

Raises:
    HTTPException 400: If reset token is invalid or expired
    HTTPException 500: If password reset fails

get.description:
Get current authenticated user profile.

Retrieves the profile of the currently authenticated user based on
their authentication token. Supports both Firebase Auth and native
email/password authentication.

For native auth users:
- Token contains uid = database user ID
- Look up user by ID directly

For Firebase auth users:
- Token contains uid = Firebase UID
- Look up user by Firebase UID, or create if first login (lazy sync)

This endpoint implements lazy user creation for Firebase Auth users.
If a user authenticates via Firebase but doesn't have a database record,
one is created automatically using data from the Firebase token.

The response includes a has_brand field indicating whether the user
has any associated brands in the system.

RMM Level 2 Compliance:
- Resource-based URL: /users/me
- HTTP method (GET) indicates read action
- No verbs in URL path
- Uses /me convention for current authenticated user

Args:
    firebase_user: Decoded token claims (injected by auth dependency)
    user_service: Injected user service

Returns:
    UserResponse with user profile data including has_brand field

Raises:
    HTTPException 401: If authentication token is missing or invalid
    HTTPException 500: If database or server error occurs

put.description:
Update current authenticated user profile.

Supports both PUT and PATCH HTTP methods. The frontend uses PATCH
for partial profile updates (Issue #3237).

Updates the profile of the currently authenticated user. Supports
updating name, email, and password. Password is hashed before storage.

RMM Level 2 Compliance:
- Resource-based URL: /users/me
- HTTP methods (PUT/PATCH) indicate update action
- No verbs in URL path
- Uses /me convention for current authenticated user

Args:
    request: Profile update data (name, email, password — all optional)
    firebase_user: Decoded token claims (injected by auth dependency)
    user_service: Injected user service

Returns:
    UserResponse with updated user profile data

Raises:
    HTTPException 401: If authentication token is missing or invalid
    HTTPException 404: If user not found
    HTTPException 500: If database or server error occurs

delete.description:
Delete current user account (GDPR Article 17).

Schedules account deletion with a 30-day grace period.
During the grace period, the user can cancel the deletion
via POST /users/me/deletion/cancellations.

After scheduling deletion:
- User data is soft-deleted (deleted_at timestamp set)
- Current access token is blacklisted (user is logged out)
- Permanent deletion occurs after 30 days

RMM Level 2 Compliance:
- DELETE to /users/me deletes the user resource
- HTTP verb (DELETE) indicates delete action
- Uses /me convention for current authenticated user
- Returns 202 Accepted (deletion is scheduled, not immediate)

Args:
    firebase_user: Decoded token claims (injected by auth dependency)
    credentials: HTTP Bearer token credentials from request header
    request: Optional deletion request with reason
    user_service: Injected user service
    token_blacklist_service: Injected token blacklist service

Returns:
    AccountDeletionResponse with deletion_date (30 days from now)

Raises:
    HTTPException 401: If authentication token is missing or invalid
    HTTPException 409: If account is already scheduled for deletion
    HTTPException 500: If deletion scheduling fails

202.description: Account deletion scheduled with 30-day grace period

409.description: Account is already scheduled for deletion

post.description:

BrandSummaryResponse.title: BrandSummaryResponse
BrandSummaryResponse.description:
Schema for brand summary response.

Issue #1386: Updated to support async pattern with optional summary.

Returns a generated cohesive brand summary paragraph based on the brand's
mission, vision, values, tone/voice, persona, industry, and vertical.

For async operations:
- summary is None (generation in progress)
- message contains status info
- correlation_id can be used for tracking

Following V1 implementation (ReviewForm.tsx):
- Generated via LLM (brand-crawler service)
- 60 words or less
- Single cohesive paragraph
- Includes: mission, vision, values, tone/voice, persona, industry, vertical
- Excludes: brand colors, service lists, location

isComplete.title: Iscomplete
isComplete.description: Whether crawl is complete and data is available

BrandSummaryView.title: BrandSummaryView
BrandSummaryView.description:
Synchronous brand summary view for Issue A (user testing feedback).

This schema extracts and displays what the system understood about the brand
after scraping. It enables users to:
- See brand attributes, tone, positioning
- Understand product focus areas
- Know what context is being passed to content generation

Extracted from the brand's crawl_data field which contains BrandAssets.

BrandUserRole.title: BrandUserRole
BrandUserRole.description:
Roles a user can have on a brand.

Ordered by privilege level (highest to lowest):
- owner: Full access, can delete brand and manage all users
- admin: Manage settings and users (except owner), cannot delete brand
- editor: Create and edit content and campaigns
- reviewer: Review and approve/reject content (cannot create)
- collaborator: Suggest edits and leave feedback (no approve rights)
- viewer: Read-only access to content and analytics

Response for campaign content generation status.

Provides high-level progress tracking for campaign content generation.
Used by frontend to poll progress during async content generation.

not percentages. The 'message' field contains the user-facing status text.

PasswordResetCompatRequest.title: PasswordResetCompatRequest
PasswordResetCompatRequest.description:
Schema for backward-compatible POST /auth/reset-password endpoint.

Issue #3207: The RMM L2 endpoint PUT /passwords/{token} takes the token
in the URL path and new_password in the body. The legacy frontend sends
both token and newPassword in the request body.

This compat schema accepts both fields from the body so the compat shim
can extract them and forward to the service layer.

email.title: Email
email.description: User email address to send reset link

PasswordResetCreateRequest.title: PasswordResetCreateRequest
PasswordResetCreateRequest.description:
Schema for creating a password reset request (RMM L2 compliant).

For POST /api/v2/passwords endpoint.
Creates a password reset token and sends email to user.

RMM Level 2 Compliance:
- Resource-based URL: /passwords (noun, not verb)
- POST method creates new password-reset resource
- No verbs in URL path

message.title: Message
message.description: Generic success message (security: doesn't reveal if email exists)
message.default: If the email exists, a password reset link has been sent.

PasswordResetCreateResponse.title: PasswordResetCreateResponse
PasswordResetCreateResponse.description:
Schema for password reset creation response (RMM L2 compliant).

Returns confirmation message without revealing if email exists.
Security: Always returns success to prevent email enumeration.

newPassword.title: Newpassword
newPassword.description: New password. Must contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one digit, and one special character.

PasswordResetUpdateRequest.title: PasswordResetUpdateRequest
PasswordResetUpdateRequest.description:
Schema for completing a password reset (RMM L2 compliant).

For PUT /api/v2/password-resets/{token} endpoint.
Updates the password using the reset token.

RMM Level 2 Compliance:
- Resource-based URL: /password-resets/{token}
- PUT method updates the password-reset resource
- Token in path (resource identifier), password in body

Password Requirements:
- Minimum 8 characters
- Maximum 128 characters
- At least one uppercase letter (A-Z)
- At least one lowercase letter (a-z)
- At least one digit (0-9)
- At least one special character (!@#$%^&*(),.?":{}|<>-_+=[]\;'`~)
- Not a commonly used password

message.title: Message
message.default: Password reset successful. You can now log in with your new password.

PasswordResetUpdateResponse.title: PasswordResetUpdateResponse
PasswordResetUpdateResponse.description:
Schema for password reset completion response (RMM L2 compliant).

Returns success message after password is reset.

SessionRequest.title: SessionRequest
SessionRequest.description:
Schema for unified session management (RMM L2 compliant).

For POST /api/v2/sessions endpoint.
Supports multiple grant types following OAuth2 patterns:
- grant_type: "password" - Login with email/password (default)
- grant_type: "refresh_token" - Refresh an existing session

RMM Level 2 Compliance:
- Single resource-based URL: /sessions
- HTTP method (POST) indicates create/update action
- Grant type in body differentiates login vs refresh
- No verbs in URL path

Examples:
    Login: {"grant_type": "password", "email": "user@example.com", "password": "secret"}
    Refresh: {"grant_type": "refresh_token", "refresh_token": "eyJ..."}

expiresIn.title: Expiresin
expiresIn.description: Token expiration time in seconds from now

user.description: User profile (included for password grant, optional for refresh)

SessionResponse.title: SessionResponse
SessionResponse.description:
Unified session response for RMM L2 compliant sessions endpoint.

Used by POST /api/v2/sessions for both login and token refresh.
Contains all fields needed for both use cases.

For password grant (login):
- user: User profile data
- access_token: JWT access token
- refresh_token: Token for refreshing session
- token_type: Always "bearer"
- expires_in: Token expiration in seconds

For refresh_token grant:
- access_token: New JWT access token
- refresh_token: New refresh token
- token_type: Always "bearer"
- expires_in: Token expiration in seconds
- user: Optional (may be omitted for refresh)

VideoJobStatusResponse.title: VideoJobStatusResponse
VideoJobStatusResponse.description:
Response schema for video job status queries.

Used by GET /api/v1/video-jobs/{job_id} endpoint (Issue #1697).
Supports "dumb frontend" pattern (Issue #2566) with pre-computed progress.

Attributes:
    job_id: UUID of the job
    post_id: ID of the associated post
    job_type: Type of video job
    status: Current status
    progress: Progress percentage 0-100 (for dumb frontend)
    estimated_time_remaining: Human-readable time estimate (for dumb frontend)
    video_url: URL of generated video (if completed)
    thumbnail_url: URL of video thumbnail (if completed)
    duration_seconds: Actual video duration (if completed)
    cost_usd: Actual cost incurred (if available)
    error_message: Error details (if failed)
    created_at: When the job was created
    completed_at: When the job completed (if finished)
